Monitoring the Impact of the Elimination of the Cap on Funds for Older Relative Caregivers, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–131, title II, § 218(b)134 Stat. 262Mar. 25, 2020“(1).—ReportNot later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act [], and annually thereafter, the Assistant Secretary [for Aging] shall submit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Education and Labor [now Committee on Education and the Workforce] of the House of Representatives a report on the impact of the amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] to eliminate the limitation on funds that States may allocate to provide support services to older relative caregivers in the National Family Caregiver Support Program established under part E of title III of the Older Americans Act of 1965 ( et seq.). Each such report shall also be made available to the public.Mar. 25, 202042 U.S.C. 3030s“(2).—ContentsFor purposes of reports required by paragraph (1), each State that receives an allotment under such National Family Caregiver Support Program for fiscal year 2020 or a subsequent fiscal year shall report to the Assistant Secretary for the fiscal year involved the amount of funds of the total Federal and non-Federal shares described in section 373(h)(2) of the Older Americans Act of 1965 () used by the State to provide support services for older relative caregivers and the amount of such funds so used for family caregivers.”42 U.S.C. 3030s–1(h)(2)

children.text: for family caregivers; and
for older relative caregivers.
information to caregivers about available services;
assistance to caregivers in gaining access to the services;

individual counseling, organization of support groups, and caregiver training to assist the caregivers in the areas of health, nutrition, and financial literacy, and in making decisions and solving problems relating to their caregiving roles;
respite care to enable caregivers to be temporarily relieved from their caregiving responsibilities; and
supplemental services, on a limited basis, to complement the care provided by caregivers.

children.label: A
children.text: are described in paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: with regard to the services specified in paragraphs (4) and (5) of subsection (b), in the case of a caregiver described in paragraph (1), is providing care to an older individual who meets the condition specified in subparagraph (A)(i) or (B) of .section 3002(22) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to caregivers who are older individuals with greatest social need, and older individuals with greatest economic need (with particular attention to low-income older individuals); and

children.label: B
children.text: to older relative caregivers of children with severe disabilities, or individuals with disabilities who have severe disabilities.

children.heading: Use of volunteers
children.label: d Use of volunteers
children.text: In carrying out this part, each area agency on aging shall make use of trained volunteers to expand the provision of the available services described in subsection (b) and, if possible, work in coordination with organizations that have experience in providing training, placement, and stipends for volunteers or participants (such as organizations carrying out Federal service programs administered by the Corporation for National and Community Service), in community service settings.

children.label: A
children.text: the use of procedures and tools to monitor and evaluate the performance of the programs carried out under such sections;

children.label: B
children.text: the use of evidence-based caregiver support services; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other issue determined relevant by the Assistant Secretary; and

make available, including on the website of the Administration and pursuant to , best practices described in paragraph (1), to carry out the programs under this section and .section 3012(a)(34) of this titlesection 3057k–11 of this title

children.heading: Quality standards and mechanisms and accountability
children.label: f Quality standards and mechanisms and accountability

children.heading: Quality standards and mechanisms
children.label: 1 Quality standards and mechanisms
children.text: The State shall establish standards and mechanisms designed to assure the quality of services provided with assistance made available under this part.

children.heading: Data and records
children.label: 2 Data and records
children.text: The State shall collect data and maintain records relating to the State program in a standardized format specified by the Assistant Secretary. The State shall furnish the records to the Assistant Secretary, at such time as the Assistant Secretary may require, in order to enable the Assistant Secretary to monitor State program administration and compliance, and to evaluate and compare the effectiveness of the State programs.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 3 Reports
children.text: The State shall prepare and submit to the Assistant Secretary reports on the data and records required under paragraph (2), including information on the services funded under this part, and standards and mechanisms, including caregiver assessments used in the State, by which the quality of the services shall be assured. The reports shall describe any mechanisms used in the State to provide to persons who are family caregivers, or older relative caregivers, information about and access to various services so that the persons can better carry out their care responsibilities.

children.label: A
children.text: From sums appropriated under for a fiscal year, the Assistant Secretary shall allot amounts among the States proportionately based on the population of individuals 70 years of age or older in the States.section 3023(e) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: In determining the amounts allotted to States from the sums appropriated under for a fiscal year, the Assistant Secretary shall first determine the amount allotted to each State under subparagraph (A) and then proportionately adjust such amounts, if necessary, to meet the requirements of paragraph (2).section 3023 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: The number of individuals 70 years of age or older in any State and in all States shall be determined by the Assistant Secretary on the basis of the most recent data available from the Bureau of the Census and other reliable demographic data satisfactory to the Assistant Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: The amounts allotted under paragraph (1) shall be reduced proportionately to the extent necessary to increase other allotments under such paragraph to achieve the amounts described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: Each State shall be allotted ½ of 1 percent of the amount appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made.

children.label: ii
children.text: Guam and the Virgin Islands of the United States shall each be allotted ¼ of 1 percent of the amount appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made.

children.label: iii
children.text: American Samoa and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands shall each be allotted ⁄ of 1 percent of the amount appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made.116

children.label: C
children.text: For the purposes of subparagraph (B)(i), the term “State” does not include Guam, American Samoa, the Virgin Islands of the United States, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: Use of funds for administration of area plans
children.label: 1 Use of funds for administration of area plans
children.text: Amounts made available to a State to carry out the State program under this part may be used, in addition to amounts available in accordance with , for costs of administration of area plans.section 3023(c)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Federal share of the cost of carrying out a State program under this part shall be 75 percent.section 3024(d)(1)(D) of this title

children.heading: Non-Federal share
children.label: B Non-Federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost shall be provided from State and local sources.

promote quality and continuous improvement in the support provided to family caregivers and older relative caregivers through programs carried out under this section and ; andsection 3057k–11 of this title
include, with respect to such programs, program evaluation, training, technical assistance, and research.

children.heading: Technical assistance for caregiver assessments
children.label: j Technical assistance for caregiver assessments

children.text: conducting caregiver assessments (including reassessments) as needed;
implementing such assessments that are consistent across a planning and service area, as appropriate; and
implementing caregiver support service plans, including conducting referrals to and coordination of activities with relevant State services.
